inference_steps: [122s to 126s] The relevant scoreboard frames are near the end of the third quarter, because the retrieved frames span 122.00s to 126.00s and OCR in those frames shows the period as "3rd" with the clock decreasing from about 0:02.8 to 0:00.0.

[122s to 126s] Across those end-of-quarter frames, the scoreboard consistently shows the team labels "BRISTOL CENT." and "E. CATHOLIC" and the score pair 52 and 40. At 126.00s, spatial grounding confirms that those labels and numbers are part of the same scoreboard overlay region.
[126s] Because the clock reaches 0:00.0 while the period still reads "3rd," the end-of-third-quarter scoreboard at 126.00s shows the score pair 52 and 40.

[126s] However, the new region-grounded evidence still does not explicitly associate 52 versus 40 to a specific team name at the 126.00s frame. Without that score-to-team mapping, the trace cannot choose between the Bristol Central leading and East Catholic leading variants, and none of the listed options exactly matches a team-agnostic reading of 52 to 40.

evidence.evidence_text: Retrieved candidate frames at 122.00s, 123.00s, 124.00s, 125.00s, and 126.00s near the end of the third quarter.

Across frames 122.00s-126.00s, OCR repeatedly reads the scoreboard with team labels "BRISTOL CENT." and "E. CATHOLIC," scores "52" and "40," period "3rd," and clocks counting down from about 0:02.8 to 0:00.0.

At frame 126.00s, the scoreboard overlay is located at the bottom of the image and contains the team names "BRISTOL CENT." and "E. CATHOLIC" together with the numeric scores "52" and "40" plus period/time information. This grounds that these labels and numbers belong to the same scoreboard region, but it does not explicitly state which number is paired with which team name.
